meta.generator: Peter (Mochaccino dark-factory workspace) — POEM consolidation decision board

frame.headline: POEM Consolidation — Decision Board
frame.thesis: YLO dies as a testing ground; its learnings consolidate into POEM, which becomes an Anthropic plugin. dark-factory gets a .poem/ folder and YLO's four workflows are rebuilt in POEM.

pillars.v: Freeze + harvest + archive. Always a testing ground.
The home. Authoring surface. Repackaged as an Anthropic plugin.
Stays the runtime (WomPackage + engine). State model simplified.
First consumer — gets a .poem/ folder, rebuilds YLO workflows in POEM.

punchlist.change: Adopt append-only log + projections + validators as the state model
punchlist.why: Kills AWB's mutability-enum complexity; audit + race-safety for free

punchlist.change: Add QuickArchitect (v1 7-step formula) as a fluid facade over Alex
punchlist.why: Restores workflow-on-demand; keeps Alex for hardening

punchlist.change: Add a new Agent Engineer; keep Penny pure Prompt Engineer
Own the workflow-definition format (move it back from the AWB boundary)
punchlist.why: Authoring belongs to the authoring layer
Distribution, hook-based enforcement, MCP tools, versioned install

punchlist.change: Drop the per-slot mutability enum; express as projection policies + validators
punchlist.why: Same guarantees, simpler substrate, shared with POEM

punchlist.change: Keep WomPackage as compiled runtime contract; consume POEM-owned definition
Freeze + harvest + archive once the .poem/ rebuild proves out
punchlist.why: It was always a testing ground
Coverage already strong — apply, don't rewrite

state_model.title: Event sourcing + projections — delete the mutability enum
state_model.claim: In event sourcing, mutability is not a storage property — it's a projection (roll-up) policy. 5 write-time mutation behaviours collapse into 5 read-time folds over one append-only log.

mutability_map.fold: last-write-wins gated by a review event

layers.name: Append-only log
layers.role: one write model — YLO's blackboard substrate (every fact immutable)

layers.name: Projection policies
layers.role: the read-side fold — replaces the mutability MODES; relational output is just a projection shape

layers.name: Write-time validators
layers.role: optional guard rails as checks over the log (e.g. immutable key folds to >1 ok-event → raise) — where AWB's 'shaped appropriately' guarantee lives

state_model.caveat: A projection changes how you READ — it can't reject a bad WRITE. That's why layer 3 (validators) exists. But validators are rules over the log, not storage modes — the substrate stays dumb.

roster.title: The agent roster + the naming snag

agents.name: Alex
agents.owns: workflow definition (heavy / compile-first) + cross-step I/O validation

agents.name: Oscar
agents.owns: runtime execution; reads workflow + prompts + data, executes

agents.name: Felix
agents.owns: logs blockers from external POEM installs back to core

new_role.collision: A is already Alex (Architect) — [R]=[N] forbids two A's
new_role.unit: input → prompt(hbs) → output  +  tools + model + capabilities
new_role.decision: keep Penny pure + add this as a NEW persona (may absorb some Penny responsibilities — TBD)

name_options.note: preferred term, but COLLIDES with Alex/Architect

name_options.name: Cara / Carl
name_options.note: free initial; 'capability' matches the expanded unit

open_questions: Agent Engineer NAME — reword the role to free an initial, or break the one-initial rule?
Which of Penny's responsibilities (if any) move to the Agent Engineer?
Projection-fold ownership — Penny (prompt level) or Alex (workflow level)?
QuickArchitect output — POEM YAML directly, or a lighter runnable form Alex upgrades?
Validator placement — plugin hook, runtime check, or compile-time check?
MCP vs skill for kie/image-gen — first-class MCP tool or Bash-shelling skill?
